appHero.title: Test @artifact/ui-lib components in a separate Vite app
appHero.description: Each section below targets a different part of the published library so visual tokens, interactivity, overlays, and controlled component behavior can be verified in one place.

navigationMove.label: Sidebar-controlled locale
navigationMove.description: Language switching now lives in the sidebar so the navigation shell tests localization, direction, and layout behavior together.

language.label: Language and locale
language.description: Powered by react-i18next so changing the language also updates direction and all static UI copy.

buttons.title: Buttons and emphasis states
buttons.description: This validates color tokens, variants, loading states, and tooltip composition.
buttons.tooltip: Tooltip rendering proves floating styles and layering work in the consumer app.

checklist.title: Integration checklist
checklist.description: Use these checks while reviewing the demo sections.

items.buttons: Buttons should render distinct brand tones and variants, not browser defaults.
items.fields: Inputs, selects, sliders, and toggles should keep rounded token-driven styling and focus states.
items.table: The data table should support search, sorting, pagination, and row selection in the consumer app.
items.feedback: Dialog and toast feedback should prove overlay, portal, and animation styles are shipped correctly.
items.direction: Switching between English and Arabic should update layout direction for the full page and portal-based UI.
items.state: Controlled values should update live inside the consumer app without prop or state mismatches.

form.title: Form controls
form.description: Controlled inputs confirm that the packaged fields work correctly with local React state.
form.emailValid: Looks valid for this smoke test.
form.emailInvalid: Enter a valid email to test the error state.
form.qaNotesMessage: Text area styling should match the rest of the field system.

selection.title: Toggles and selection controls
selection.description: This section stresses checkbox, switch, radio, and slider behavior with live state updates.

emailAlerts.label: Email alerts
emailAlerts.message: Tests checkbox label, hint text, and checked styling.

pushAlerts.label: Push alerts
pushAlerts.message: Uses the same control with a separate local state path.

canary.label: Canary
canary.message: Fastest iteration path for smoke testing.

selection.qualityMessage: Move the thumb to verify track, range, and value label rendering.

publishReady.label: Publish ready
publishReady.message: Switch styles should react cleanly across checked, unchecked, and focus states.

table.title: Data table example
table.description: This uses the packaged table component with client-side search, sorting, pagination, and row selection so you can verify layout behavior in both English and Arabic.
table.currentDirection: Current direction: {{direction}}. Switch the page language and confirm table alignment, search, pagination, and row selection still feel correct.
table.searchEmptyState: No results matched the current search.

feedback.title: Overlays and feedback
feedback.description: Dialog and toast are useful consumer-side checks because they depend on portals, layering, transitions, and global styling.
feedback.notice: Open the review dialog, then confirm it. You should see a styled modal, a dimmed overlay, and toast notifications that match the library design language.

toasts.reviewOpenedDescription: Overlay, portal, and toast layers are now active in the consumer app.
toasts.successDescription: The smoke test confirms feedback primitives respond correctly.
toasts.warningDescription: This verifies standalone toast calls outside dialog interactions.

dialog.title: External project dialog test
dialog.description: If this modal opens with a styled backdrop and surfaced panel, the published overlay components are working correctly in this consumer application.
dialog.body: This action simulates a QA sign-off flow using only the installed package components.

brand.title: Header and sidebar smoke test
brand.description: This shell verifies the packaged navigation components alongside the rest of the UI library.

header.title: Consumer app navigation
header.description: Header chips, trigger, live clock, and current date all come from the published package.

language.label: Language switcher
language.description: Switch the entire app language from here to verify RTL and LTR behavior inside the navigation shell.
